generated_note: Counseling effectiveness pipeline — data from generate_counseling_dashboard_data.py

insights.headline: 12% of sessions (334) were detected as needing attention. That equals 58 unique residents, averaging 5.8 flagged sessions per resident.
insights.lede: Use 35% as the follow-up line. This catches more at-risk sessions, but it also creates extra check-ins. About 3% of sessions without a later concern will still be flagged.

prediction_cards.label: Sessions marked for supervisor review
prediction_cards.hint: 11.8% of all sessions scored at 35% concern or higher
prediction_cards.definition: Count of sessions whose model concern score meets the follow-up threshold in the current data slice.

prediction_cards.label: Mean concern probability across sessions
prediction_cards.hint: Typical risk level before applying the 35% follow-up line
prediction_cards.definition: Average predicted concern probability across sessions in view (before applying the binary flag rule).

prediction_cards.label: Residents with at least one flagged session
prediction_cards.hint: Averaging 5.8 flagged sessions per affected resident
prediction_cards.definition: Unique residents with at least one session at or above the follow-up threshold.

prediction_cards.kicker: Flags vs recorded concerns (predictive vs records)
prediction_cards.label: Share of real concerns the flag line caught early
prediction_cards.hint: 265 of 677 sessions that later had a concern note were already flagged
prediction_cards.definition: Of sessions that later had a concern in case notes, what share were already flagged by the model line.

prediction_cards.label: Average mood change after sessions
prediction_cards.hint: Positive means residents generally ended sessions in a better state
prediction_cards.definition: Mean change in recorded mood from start to end of session (scale from source data).

cause_cards.kicker: Starting mood vs later concern (from records)
cause_cards.title: Highest-risk starting mood: Withdrawn
cause_cards.body: 28% of sessions starting here had a concern filed later.
cause_cards.definition: Compares later recorded concerns by starting mood group—association from records, not causal proof.

cause_cards.kicker: Session format vs concern rate (from records)
cause_cards.title: Individual sessions show higher concern rates than Group
cause_cards.definition: Share of sessions with a recorded concern, split by session format in this export.

cause_cards.title: How often concerns show up in case notes
cause_cards.body: 24% of sessions (677 total) have a concern recorded—this is the real outcome, not the prediction.
cause_cards.definition: Baseline rate of concerns documented in case notes across all sessions (ground truth, not the model).

cause_cards.kicker: How to read the pattern cards
cause_cards.title: Association is not the same as cause
cause_cards.body: These compare groups after the fact. They help supervisors notice themes; they do not prove that one factor alone caused a concern.
cause_cards.definition: Explains how to interpret pattern cards: descriptive comparisons only.
